items.title: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement
items.whyInNews: The Union Minister of Commerce and Industry, Piyush Goyal, and New Zealand's Minister of Trade and Investment, Todd McClay, held discussions with industry leaders in Agra on April 26, 2026, ahead of the signing of the India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
items.summary: The India-New Zealand FTA aims to boost bilateral trade between the two countries. The agreement is expected to increase trade in goods and services, and promote investment. The FTA will also provide a framework for cooperation in areas such as customs procedures, trade facilitation, and dispute settlement. India and New Zealand have been negotiating the FTA since 2010, and the agreement is expected to be signed soon.
keyPoints: India and New Zealand are set to sign a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) to boost bilateral trade
The FTA will increase trade in goods and services, and promote investment
The agreement will provide a framework for cooperation in areas such as customs procedures and trade facilitation
India and New Zealand have been negotiating the FTA since 2010
importantPoints: Full form of FTA is Free Trade Agreement, which was first introduced in 1947, and its headquarters is in Geneva, Switzerland
The FTA is related to Article 301 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with freedom of trade and commerce
This is the first FTA between India and New Zealand, and it is expected to increase bilateral trade to $2 billion by 2025
India's trade deficit with New Zealand was $1.3 billion in 2022, and the FTA is expected to reduce this deficit
The FTA will have a significant impact on India's economy, as it will increase trade and investment between the two countries
items.examRelevance: Economy: International Trade, specifically the chapter on Free Trade Agreements

items.title: National Workshop on Social Justice
items.whyInNews: The National Workshop on Social Justice was held in Chandigarh from April 24-26, 2026, with the aim of promoting social justice and equality in India. The workshop was attended by officials from various government departments, NGOs, and civil society organizations.
items.summary: The National Workshop on Social Justice was organized by the Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment, and it aimed to promote social justice and equality in India. The workshop discussed various issues related to social justice, including poverty, inequality, and discrimination. The participants also discussed ways to promote social justice and equality, including through education, healthcare, and economic empowerment.
keyPoints: The National Workshop on Social Justice was held in Chandigarh from April 24-26, 2026
The workshop aimed to promote social justice and equality in India
The workshop discussed various issues related to social justice, including poverty and inequality
The participants discussed ways to promote social justice and equality, including through education and healthcare
importantPoints: Full form of NGO is Non-Governmental Organization, which was first introduced in 1945, and its headquarters is in New York, USA
The workshop is related to Article 14 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with equality before the law
This is the first national workshop on social justice, and it is expected to promote social justice and equality in India
India's ranking in the Human Development Index (HDI) is 131 out of 189 countries, and the workshop aimed to improve this ranking
The workshop will have a significant impact on India's social sector, as it will promote social justice and equality
items.examRelevance: Polity: Social Justice, specifically the chapter on Social Justice and Equality

items.title: India's Wheat Production
items.whyInNews: The government has clarified the wheat production scenario for 2025-26, stating that despite changes in weather patterns, the crop remains strong. The government has taken various measures to support wheat farmers, including providing subsidies and improving irrigation facilities.
items.summary: The government has clarified the wheat production scenario for 2025-26, stating that despite changes in weather patterns, the crop remains strong. The government has taken various measures to support wheat farmers, including providing subsidies and improving irrigation facilities. The government has also implemented various schemes to promote wheat production, including the National Food Security Mission and the Rashtriya Krishi Vikas Yojana.
keyPoints: The government has clarified the wheat production scenario for 2025-26
Despite changes in weather patterns, the wheat crop remains strong
The government has taken various measures to support wheat farmers, including providing subsidies
The government has implemented various schemes to promote wheat production, including the National Food Security Mission
importantPoints: Full form of NFSA is National Food Security Act, which was introduced in 2013, and its headquarters is in New Delhi, India
The wheat production scenario is related to Article 47 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the duty of the state to raise the level of nutrition and the standard of living
India is the second-largest producer of wheat in the world, and the crop is a major source of income for farmers
The government's measures to support wheat farmers are expected to increase wheat production to 100 million tonnes by 2025
The wheat production scenario will have a significant impact on India's food security, as wheat is a staple crop
items.examRelevance: Economy: Agriculture, specifically the chapter on Food Security and Wheat Production

items.title: India's Space Programme
items.whyInNews: The government has announced plans to establish space laboratories in universities and colleges across India, with the aim of promoting space research and education. The first phase of the project will see the establishment of seven space laboratories.
items.summary: The government has announced plans to establish space laboratories in universities and colleges across India, with the aim of promoting space research and education. The first phase of the project will see the establishment of seven space laboratories, which will be equipped with state-of-the-art facilities and equipment. The laboratories will provide opportunities for students and researchers to conduct space-related research and experiments.
keyPoints: The government has announced plans to establish space laboratories in universities and colleges across India
The first phase of the project will see the establishment of seven space laboratories
The laboratories will be equipped with state-of-the-art facilities and equipment
The laboratories will provide opportunities for students and researchers to conduct space-related research and experiments
importantPoints: Full form of ISRO is Indian Space Research Organisation, which was established in 1969, and its headquarters is in Bengaluru, India
The space laboratories are related to Article 51 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the promotion of international peace and security
India has made significant progress in space research and exploration, including the successful launch of the Mangalyaan mission to Mars
The establishment of space laboratories will provide opportunities for students and researchers to conduct space-related research and experiments
The space laboratories will have a significant impact on India's space programme, as they will promote space research and education
items.examRelevance: Science: Space Research, specifically the chapter on Indian Space Programme

items.title: Ganga Expressway Inauguration
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the Ganga Expressway in Uttar Pradesh on April 29, 2026. The expressway is the longest in Uttar Pradesh and will connect the cities of Prayagraj and Varanasi.
items.summary: The Ganga Expressway is a significant infrastructure project in Uttar Pradesh, and its inauguration will mark a major milestone in the state's development. The expressway will connect the cities of Prayagraj and Varanasi, and will provide a major boost to the state's economy. The expressway will also improve connectivity and reduce travel time between the two cities.
keyPoints: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the Ganga Expressway in Uttar Pradesh on April 29, 2026
The expressway is the longest in Uttar Pradesh and will connect the cities of Prayagraj and Varanasi
The expressway will provide a major boost to the state's economy
The expressway will improve connectivity and reduce travel time between the two cities
importantPoints: Full form of NHAI is National Highways Authority of India, which was established in 1988, and its headquarters is in New Delhi, India
The Ganga Expressway is related to Article 356 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the provisions in case of failure of constitutional machinery in states
The expressway is a significant infrastructure project in Uttar Pradesh, and its inauguration will mark a major milestone in the state's development
The expressway will have a significant impact on the state's economy, as it will improve connectivity and reduce travel time between the two cities
The expressway will also provide a major boost to the state's tourism industry, as it will improve access to the cities of Prayagraj and Varanasi
items.examRelevance: Economy: Infrastructure, specifically the chapter on Transportation and Connectivity

items.title: NSA Doval discusses regional situation with UAE President Al Nahyan
items.whyInNews: National Security Advisor Ajit Doval met with UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan on April 27, 2026, to discuss measures to deepen the comprehensive strategic partnership and the regional situation.
items.summary: The meeting between NSA Ajit Doval and UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan took place on April 27, 2026, in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates. They discussed ways to strengthen the bilateral relationship between India and the UAE. The meeting was significant as it came at a time when the region is witnessing several geopolitical developments.
keyPoints: NSA Ajit Doval met UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan on April 27, 2026
The meeting took place in Abu Dhabi, United Arab Emirates
They discussed measures to deepen the comprehensive strategic partnership
The regional situation was also discussed during the meeting
importantPoints: UAE stands for United Arab Emirates, founded in 1971, with its headquarters in Abu Dhabi
The meeting is related to Article 51 of the Indian Constitution, which deals with the promotion of international peace and security
This is the first time NSA Ajit Doval has met with UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan in 2026
The meeting is significant as it reflects the growing strategic partnership between India and the UAE
The partnership has implications for India's foreign policy and regional security

items.title: Sanju Samson reaches 5,000 runs in IPL
items.whyInNews: Sanju Samson reached the milestone of 5,000 runs in the Indian Premier League on April 26, 2026, during a match between Rajasthan Royals and Delhi Capitals.
items.summary: Sanju Samson achieved the milestone of 5,000 runs in the Indian Premier League on April 26, 2026, at the Arun Jaitley Stadium in Delhi. He became the 12th batsman to reach this milestone in the IPL. Sanju Samson has played 180 innings in the IPL, scoring 5008 runs at an average of 31.69 and a strike rate of 140.59.
keyPoints: Sanju Samson reached 5,000 runs in IPL on April 26, 2026
He achieved the milestone during a match between Rajasthan Royals and Delhi Capitals
Sanju Samson has played 180 innings in the IPL
He has scored 5008 runs at an average of 31.69 and a strike rate of 140.59
importantPoints: IPL stands for Indian Premier League, founded in 2008, with its headquarters in Mumbai
The milestone is related to the sports sector, specifically cricket
Sanju Samson is the 12th batsman to reach 5,000 runs in the IPL
The achievement reflects Sanju Samson's consistency and performance in the IPL
The milestone has implications for Sanju Samson's career and the IPL

items.title: Uber Cup: India revive quarterfinal hopes with dominant win over Ukraine
items.whyInNews: The Indian badminton team defeated Ukraine 3-0 in the Uber Cup on April 26, 2026, to revive their quarterfinal hopes.
items.summary: The Indian badminton team defeated Ukraine 3-0 in the Uber Cup on April 26, 2026, at the Impact Arena in Bangkok, Thailand. Unnati Hooda, Tanvi Sharma, and the pair of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand won their respective matches to give India a dominant victory. The win has revived India's quarterfinal hopes in the tournament.
keyPoints: India defeated Ukraine 3-0 in the Uber Cup on April 26, 2026
The match took place at the Impact Arena in Bangkok, Thailand
Unnati Hooda, Tanvi Sharma, and the pair of Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand won their matches
The win has revived India's quarterfinal hopes in the tournament
importantPoints: Uber Cup is an international badminton tournament, founded in 1956, with its headquarters in Kuala Lumpur
The tournament is related to the sports sector, specifically badminton
India's win has given them a chance to qualify for the quarterfinals
The Indian team's performance reflects their skills and strategy in the tournament
The win has implications for India's ranking and reputation in international badminton
